foundational_primitive: Hopf bundle S¹ → S³ → S² (BAM angular base, #59–#66)
D5_minimality: minimal D above 4D spacetime with squared metric (matches spin-½ double cover)

tests.name: T1_hopf_bundle_foundational_primitive
tests.description: Hopf bundle S¹ → S³ → S² is BAM's foundational angular structure: gives the spin-½ monopole (A_φ = ½ cos χ), the double-cover spinor (T² = −I, B2), the Wigner rotation (#60), CPT (#65), the throat Dirac spinor (#66). dim(S³) = 3 is the foundational primitive.

tests.name: T2_k_5_equals_D_bulk
tests.description: BAM bulk = time × radial × S³; D_bulk = 1 + 1 + dim(S³) = 1 + 1 + 3 = 5. Identification: k_5 = D_bulk = 5.

tests.name: T3_tangherlini_D5_features
tests.description: All four Tangherlini-D=5 features in V_tangherlini: metric power D−3=2 (f(r)=1−(rs/r)²), centrifugal l(l+D−3)=l(l+2), throat curvature (D−2)=3·rs²/r⁴, ε's k_5⁴=625 denominator. All trace to D = 5.

tests.name: T4_D5_minimal_squared_metric
tests.description: Tangherlini metric f(r) = 1 − (rs/r)^(D−3): D=4 linear, D=5 squared (BAM, matches spin-½ double cover / T²=−I, B2), D=6 cubed. D=5 is the MINIMAL bulk above 4D spacetime with the squared metric.

tests.name: T5_unification_across_lepton_sector
tests.description: Same k_5 = D_bulk = 5: β_lepton = k_5²·(2π) = 50π (#71), #gen = (k_5+1)/2 = 3 (#72), ε = 7π/(100·k_5⁴) (hbar_origin). All trace to the same primitive.

tests.name: T6_structural_chain
tests.description: Hopf bundle → S³ → D_bulk = 5 → k_5 = 5 → all BAM lepton-sector structural results. Reduces 'why k_5 = 5' to 'why the Hopf bundle as angular base' — BAM's foundational ansatz.

tests.name: T7_honest_scope_b4
tests.description: Structural identification k_5 = D_bulk = dim(S³) + 2 = 5, given the Hopf bundle as BAM's angular base (foundational primitive, established across #59–#66). Does NOT derive 'why the Hopf bundle' from no assumptions. B4: dimensionless integers; structural/topological; scale-independent.
tests.derives: k_5 = 5 from D_bulk = dim(S³) + 2 (given Hopf bundle)
tests.does_not_derive: why the Hopf bundle / S³ as angular base (BAM ansatz)

tests.name: T8_assessment
tests.description: k_5 = 5 = D_bulk = dim(S³) + 2: the BAM bulk dimension (time × radial × S³). A single primitive underlies β_lepton (#71), #gen (#72), ε (hbar_origin), and the V_tangherlini structure. D=5 is minimal above 4D spacetime with squared metric, matching the spin-½ double cover (B2).
tests.D5_minimality: minimal D above 4D spacetime with squared metric
tests.remaining: first-principles "why the Hopf bundle / S³" (the foundational ansatz)

verdict:
k_5 = 5 = D_bulk = dim(S³) + 2. The BAM bulk decomposes as time × radial × S³, with S³ the Hopf bundle's total space (BAM's foundational angular structure, established across the spin/CPT arc #59–#66 as the structure that gives spin-½, the monopole connection, Wigner rotation, CPT, and the throat Dirac spinor). Therefore k_5 = D_bulk = 1 + 1 + 3 = 5.

TANGHERLINI-D=5 STRUCTURE. Every BAM Tangherlini feature in V_tangherlini traces to D = 5: the squared metric f(r) = 1 − (rs/r)² (power D−3 = 2), the centrifugal l(l+2) (S³ Casimir l(l+D−3)), the throat curvature 3·rs²/r⁴ (coefficient D−2 = 3), and the k_5⁴ = 625 denominator of ε = 7π/(100·k_5⁴).

D=5 MINIMALITY. Tangherlini in D dimensions has f(r) = 1 − (rs/r)^(D−3): D=4 (Schwarzschild) gives the linear (rs/r); D=5 gives the SQUARED (rs/r)², which matches BAM's spin-½ double cover (T² = −I, B2). D=5 is the MINIMAL bulk above 4D spacetime giving the squared metric.

UNIFICATION. The same k_5 = 5 primitive underlies the entire lepton-sector structural arc: β_lepton = k_5²·(2π) = 50π (#71), #generations = (k_5+1)/2 = 3 (#72), and ε = 7π/(100·k_5⁴) (hbar_origin). One primitive, all the "5"s.

THE CHAIN. Hopf bundle → S³ → D_bulk = 5 → k_5 = 5 → β_lepton, #gen, ε, V_tangherlini. The "why k_5 = 5" question reduces to "why the Hopf bundle as the angular base" — BAM's foundational primitive, set by the spin-½ requirement and independently established across the spin/CPT arc.

HONEST SCOPE. Identifies k_5 = D_bulk via dim(S³) = 3 + the time/radial extension; does NOT derive "why the Hopf bundle / S³" from no assumptions — that's the foundational BAM ansatz. B4: k_5 and dim(S³) are dimensionless integers; the structural identifications are topological — scale-independent. The mass values carry the scale, the topological charge does not.
